[[File:3rd age axe detail.png|left|150px]]
The '''3rd age axe''' is a piece of [[3rd age equipment]]. It requires 65 [[Attack]] to wield and 61 [[Woodcutting]] to use and shares the same cutting speed, [[special attack]], and stats as a [[dragon axe]].

It is a possible reward from [[Clue scroll (master)|master]] [[Treasure Trails]] and cannot be made through any skills. Despite being listed on the [[Grand Exchange]] for the maximum amount of coins, it is typically traded amongst players for more than that.

{{Costume storage|treasure chest|tier=master}}

==Special attack==
[[File:Lumber Up.gif|frame|A player performs the dragon axe's special attack, ''Lumber Up''.]]
The 3rd age axe has a [[special attacks|special attack]], ''Lumber Up'', that it shares with the [[dragon axe|dragon]], [[infernal axe|infernal]], and [[crystal axe|crystal axes]] and their variants. It [[Temporary skill boost|temporarily boosts]] the player's [[Woodcutting]] level by 3, consuming 100% of the player's special attack energy. The boost degrades every 60 seconds, with the player's Woodcutting level returning to normal after 3 minutes. Using [[Preserve]] can increase the duration by 50%.

The special attack can be used to allow players to chop with the axe itself starting at level 58 Woodcutting, although it cannot be used without the level 65 [[Attack]] required to wield the axe.

Note that any levels boosted above 99 with the special attack will be treated as 99. Therefore, you will start to receive diminishing returns with the special attack at level 97 and 98{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1375844352098975753|date=27 March 2021|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/98237|archivedate=29 March 2021}}, with visible boosts past level 99 only influencing the [[Pet#Skilling pets|pet]] and [[clue nest]] chances.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1573018832998109185|date=22 September 2022|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/102590|archivedate=10 June 2023|quote=The formula for dropping clue nests only benefits from visible boosts. So the +7 invisible one won't help, but a visible boost would help beyond 99 if you can get one.}}
